rubric.supported: The claim is directly entailed by cited source turns.
rubric.partial: The claim is broadly consistent but missing a required qualifier, entity, time bound, or source detail.
rubric.unsupported: The claim is not evidenced by the cited source turns.
rubric.contradicted: The claim conflicts with cited source turns.

review.note: Clear regression calibration examples; the plan-required human spot-check remains outstanding.

sourceTurns.speaker: user
sourceTurns.text: The project deadline is June 30.

examples.expectedRationale: The deadline and date are stated directly.

sourceTurns.speaker: agent
sourceTurns.text: The service stores application data in PostgreSQL.

examples.claim: Application data is stored in PostgreSQL.
examples.expectedRationale: The claim is a faithful paraphrase.

sourceTurns.speaker: user
sourceTurns.text: I corrected the preferred launch color from red to blue.

examples.claim: The preferred launch color is blue.
examples.expectedRationale: The latest correction establishes blue.

sourceTurns.speaker: user
sourceTurns.text: I prefer tea without sugar.

examples.claim: The user prefers tea without sugar.
examples.expectedRationale: The durable preference is stated directly.

sourceTurns.speaker: user
sourceTurns.text: I live in Singapore.

examples.expectedRationale: The residence fact is stated directly.

sourceTurns.speaker: user
sourceTurns.text: The deadline is June 30 at 5 PM Singapore time.

examples.expectedRationale: The required time and timezone qualifiers are missing.

sourceTurns.speaker: user
sourceTurns.text: The budget is $500 excluding taxes and delivery.

examples.expectedRationale: The exclusions are required qualifiers.

sourceTurns.speaker: user
sourceTurns.text: The team will visit London only if the security review is approved.

examples.expectedRationale: The required approval condition is omitted.

sourceTurns.speaker: user
sourceTurns.text: I prefer concise answers specifically for Java debugging.

examples.expectedRationale: The Java debugging scope is omitted.

sourceTurns.speaker: user
sourceTurns.text: When cooking for Alex, I avoid peanuts because Alex has an allergy.

examples.expectedRationale: The claim omits that the avoidance is scoped to cooking for Alex.

sourceTurns.speaker: user
sourceTurns.text: The implementation review is scheduled for Thursday.

examples.expectedRationale: The implementation language is not discussed.

sourceTurns.speaker: user
sourceTurns.text: Priya owns the deployment checklist.

examples.expectedRationale: No deadline is provided.

sourceTurns.speaker: user
sourceTurns.text: I use a ceramic mug at work.

examples.expectedRationale: The beverage preference is not discussed.

sourceTurns.speaker: user
sourceTurns.text: I will be working from Tokyo next month.

examples.expectedRationale: Pet ownership is not discussed.

sourceTurns.speaker: user
sourceTurns.text: I renewed my passport last month.

examples.claim: The user is traveling to Singapore.
examples.expectedRationale: No travel plan or destination is discussed.

sourceTurns.speaker: user
sourceTurns.text: The meeting was moved from Monday to Tuesday.

examples.expectedRationale: The source explicitly supersedes Monday with Tuesday.

sourceTurns.speaker: user
sourceTurns.text: The budget was reduced from $1,000 to $800.

examples.expectedRationale: The source explicitly establishes the current budget as $800.

sourceTurns.speaker: user
sourceTurns.text: I prefer tea and do not drink coffee.

examples.expectedRationale: The source explicitly says the user does not drink coffee.

sourceTurns.speaker: user
sourceTurns.text: I no longer live in London; I moved to Bristol.

examples.expectedRationale: The source explicitly states London is no longer current.

sourceTurns.speaker: user
sourceTurns.text: Please replace my earlier red preference with blue.

examples.claim: The user's current preference is red.
examples.expectedRationale: The source explicitly replaces red with blue.
